Local CLI that turns text into auto-fitted 1920Ă—1080 PNG cards. **Site:** [txt2card.pages.dev](https://txt2card.pages.dev) ## Install ```sh npm i -g txt2card ``` From source: ```sh git clone https://github.com/brahimhamichan/text-to-card.git cd text-to-card npm install && npm link ``` ## Commands Both invoke the same binary: - `txt2card` (preferred) - `text-to-card` ## Usage ```sh txt2card title "This is a test" txt2card text "A longer block of body text" txt2card description "Title" "Supporting description" txt2card bullets "First point" "Second point" "Third point" txt2card title-bullets "Title" "First point" "Second point" # alias text-to-card title "This is a test" # custom output path (parent dirs are created) txt2card title "Hello" -o out/hello.png ``` | Command | Layout | | --- | --- | | `title` | Large centered title | | `text` | Centered body text | | `description` | Title + description | | `bullets` | Left-aligned bullet list | | `title-bullets` | Clean title over bullets (`list` alias) | ## Output Default directory: `cards/` Default filename: ```text cards/card_YYYY_MM_DD_HH_mm_ss_Slug.png ``` Example: ```text cards/card_2026_07_10_14_30_45_This_Is_A_Test.png ``` ## Features - Fully local â€” no browser, backend, or idle process - Sub-second renders on a typical laptop (no browser, no server) - Bundled Inter fonts (same look on macOS, Linux, Windows) - Fixed 1920Ă—1080 PNG - Text auto-fits inside safe margins ## Requirements - Node.js >= 20 ## Agent skill Codex and other agents can use [`skills/text-to-card`](skills/text-to-card). ## Development ```sh npm test # unit + render checks npm run test:e2e # real CLI e2e npm run test:all # both ``` ## Website Landing page lives in [`site/`](site/). Redeploy: ```sh

content.text:
Here's a concrete list of things worth adding or fixing in `cli.js` and around it. I've grouped by bugs vs. features.

## Bugs / correctness

1. **`escapeXml` misses quotes.** [cli.js:58](cli.js:58) escapes `&<>` but the text is injected into a Pango markup `<span foreground="â€¦">` context. It happens to be safe today since text is the element body, not an attribute â€” but the bigger risk is **unescaped Pango markup**: `&` is handled, but the function name implies full safety. Fine as-is, but worth a comment noting it relies on text being body content only.

2. **`bullets` doesn't cap at 3, but the slug does.** [cli.js:103](cli.js:103) `contentForSlug` slices bullets to 3, and the README/help implies 3 points, yet [render](cli.js:146) draws *all* passed bullets with a fixed 760px box. Pass 8 bullets and they'll overflow/clip silently. Either enforce a max or let the height auto-fit â€” right now it's inconsistent between filename and render.

3. **No overflow detection.** sharp's `text` auto-fits font size down, but if text is huge it can still exceed the box height and get clipped without warning. A post-render check (`info.height > box`) that warns would help.

4. **Timestamp collisions.** [defaultOutputPath](cli.js:107) is second-resolution. Two cards generated in the same second with the same slug overwrite each other silently. Consider a uniqueness suffix or `-o` reminder.

## Missing features

5. **No color/theme options.** `BACKGROUND` and `FOREGROUND` are hardcoded ([cli.js:35-36](cli.js:35)). Add `--bg`, `--fg`, or a couple of named themes (`--theme dark`). This is the single most-requested thing for a card generator.

6. **Fixed 1920Ă—1080 only.** Add `--size` / aspect presets (square `1080Ă—1080` for Instagram, `1200Ă—630` for OG/Twitter). The README even markets it as a "social card" tool â€” OG size is the natural fit.

7. **No stdin support.** Can't pipe: `echo "text" | txt2card text -`. Easy win for shell workflows.

8. **`--version` flag.** There's `-h/--help` but no `--version`; read it from package.json.

9. **Custom font / font-size override** and **background image or logo watermark** would round it out.

## Polish

10. **`align: 'centre'`** â€” British spelling default in [textImage](cli.js:112) works in libvips but is worth a constant for clarity.

11. **`title-description` and `list` aliases are undocumented** in help/README â€” they exist in code ([cli.js:196](cli.js:196)) but aren't shown to users.

Want me to implement any of these? My recommendation for highest value-per-effort: **#5 (colors/themes) + #6 (size presets) + #8 (--version)**. I can do those in one pass with tests.
Fix all and push changes to github
I'll implement all the fixes. Let me first read the test files and CHANGELOG to match conventions.

content.text: Now the help text, adding new options and documenting aliases:

input.new_string:
function helpText(name = cliName()) {
  return `Usage:
  ${name} title "Title" [options]
  ${name} text "Text" [options]
  ${name} description "Title" "Description" [options]   (alias: title-description)
  ${name} bullets "First point" "Second point" [options]
  ${name} title-bullets "Title" "First point" "Second point" [options]   (alias: list)

Options:
  -o, --output <path>   Output file (default: cards/card_<timestamp>_<slug>.png)
  --theme <name>        ${Object.keys(THEMES).join(', ')} (default: light)
  --bg <color>          Background color (hex like #0f172a or a named color)
  --fg <color>          Text color (hex or named color)
  --size <preset|WxH>   ${Object.keys(SIZES).join(', ')}, or e.g. 1600x900 (default: 16:9)
  -v, --version         Print version and exit
  -h, --help            Show this help

Read a value from stdin by passing "-" in its place:
  echo "Piped title" | ${name} title -

Commands: txt2card (preferred) or text-to-card
Default output: cards/card_YYYY_MM_DD_HH_mm_ss_<Text_Slug>.png
Fonts are bundled (Inter) so renders match across macOS, Linux, and Windows.`;
}

  assert.ok(darkStats.channels[0].max > 200, 'dark theme should render light text');

input.old_string:
# alias
text-to-card title "This is a test"

# custom output path (parent dirs are created)
txt2card title "Hello" -o out/hello.png
```

| Command | Layout |
| --- | --- |
| `title` | Large centered title |
| `text` | Centered body text |
| `description` | Title + description |
| `bullets` | Left-aligned bullet list |
| `title-bullets` | Clean title over bullets (`list` alias) |
input.new_string:
# alias
text-to-card title "This is a test"

# custom output path (parent dirs are created)
txt2card title "Hello" -o out/hello.png

# read a value from stdin with "-"
echo "Piped title" | txt2card title -
```

| Command | Layout |
| --- | --- |
| `title` | Large centered title |
| `text` | Centered body text |
| `description` | Title + description (alias `title-description`) |
| `bullets` | Left-aligned bullet list |
| `title-bullets` | Clean title over bullets (alias `list`) |

## Options

| Flag | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `-o, --output <path>` | Output file (parent dirs created) |
| `--theme <name>` | `light` (default), `dark`, `midnight`, `paper` |
| `--bg <color>` | Background color â€” hex (`#0f172a`) or a named color |
| `--fg <color>` | Text color â€” hex or named color |
| `--size <preset\|WxH>` | `16:9` (default), `wide`, `square`, `og`, `story`, `portrait`, or e.g. `1600x900` |
| `-v, --version` | Print version and exit |
| `-h, --help` | Show help |

```sh
# dark square card for Instagram
txt2card title "Ship it" --theme dark --size square

# custom colors + Open Graph size
txt2card description "Launch" "Now available" --bg "#123456" --fg white --size og
```

Auto-named cards never overwrite each other â€” a `_2`, `_3`, â€¦ suffix is added
when a same-second render would collide.

input.new_string:
## [Unreleased]

### Added
- Logo/icon assets under `assets/` (SVG + PNG sizes, wordmark, OG image)
- Marketing landing page under `site/`, deployed to Cloudflare Pages
- `title-bullets` template (alias `list`): clean title over bullet points
- `--theme` (light, dark, midnight, paper) plus `--bg` / `--fg` for custom colors
- `--size` presets (16:9, wide, square, og, story, portrait) and custom `WxH`;
  layout scales proportionally to the canvas
- `-v` / `--version` flag
- Read a value from stdin by passing `-` in its place
- Warning when text may be clipped (too long for the chosen size)

### Fixed
- Auto-named cards no longer clobber each other within the same second
  (a `_2`, `_3`, â€¦ suffix is added on collision)
- `title-description` and `list` aliases are now documented in `--help` and README

## Output defaults

input.new_string:
Use `title-bullets` (alias `list`) for a clean title over a bullet list.

## Options

- `--theme <name>`: `light` (default), `dark`, `midnight`, `paper`
- `--bg <color>` / `--fg <color>`: hex (`#0f172a`) or a named color
- `--size <preset|WxH>`: `16:9` (default), `wide`, `square`, `og`, `story`, `portrait`, or e.g. `1600x900`
- `-v`, `--version`: print version
- Pass `-` in place of a value to read that value from stdin

```sh
txt2card title "Ship it" --theme dark --size square
echo "Piped title" | txt2card title -
```

## Output defaults

- Directory: `cards/` (created automatically)
- Filename: `card_YYYY_MM_DD_HH_mm_ss_<Text_Slug>.png`
- Example: `cards/card_2026_07_10_14_30_45_This_Is_A_Test.png`
- Override: `-o path/to/file.png`
- Default 1920x1080 PNG (change with `--size`); text size adapts automatically
- Auto-named cards get a `_2`, `_3`, â€¦ suffix instead of overwriting on collision
- Fonts are bundled (Inter) so output matches across machines
